meta.our_scoring_note: XinceAI is operational: 9 pre-built workflows live, JR+ pre-approved consultant status via Elitez Group, founder-led delivery model, Xero SG / PayNow / IRAS native. PSG application not yet submitted (target Q3 2026). Big-4-grade workforce-redesign consulting wraps via Elitez Group senior partners, not yet in-platform. Cross-border (MY/ID/VN) tax + employment templating not built. Scores reflect shipped-or-imminent capability, not aspiration.

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: XinceAI breaks the cluster on jr_plus_eligibility (zero competing AI workflow vendor in the SG SME tier holds WSG JR+ pre-approval) and pre_built_workflow_library (Zapier / Make / n8n require build-your-own; SG SMEs without a tech lead stall after 2 builds), at the deliberate cost of regional_rollout_reach where Zapier and Deel-class platforms continue to win SEA + global multi-jurisdiction mandates.

dimensions.label: IMDA PSG / EDG / SFEC grant fluency
Pre-built agentic workflow library (no DIY required)
SG localisation depth (PayNow, GST, IRAS, MOM, CPF)
Deployment speed (live in days, not quarters)

rubrics.jr_plus_eligibility: 0 — Foreign vendor with no SG entity; zero awareness of WSG JR+. 1 — SG presence but not on JR+ pre-approved register. 2 — Adjacent capability (training, consulting) with WSG history but not JR+. 3 — JR+ application in flight; no LOA yet. 4 — JR+ pre-approved consultant on register; full Component 1+2+3 delivery capability. 5 — JR+ pre-approved AND a dedicated JRET/HCDT delivery framework AND ≥3 completed JR+ projects with documented JRET uplift.
rubrics.psg_grant_eligibility: 0 — No SG grant posture; foreign-currency billing only. 1 — SG entity but no grant pre-approval. 2 — One grant pre-approved (PSG OR EDG). 3 — Two grants pre-approved. 4 — Three grants pre-approved (PSG + EDG + SFEC). 5 — All four (PSG + EDG + SFEC + JR+) with single-application BGP workflow.
rubrics.pre_built_workflow_library: 0 — Pure custom-build / DIY platform (Zapier-style). 1 — Templates library, but each instantiation is bespoke. 2 — 3–5 pre-built workflows shipped. 3 — 6–8 pre-built workflows shipped. 4 — 9+ pre-built workflows shipped + workflow library is a primary nav item. 5 — 9+ workflows shipped AND industry-vertical packs (F&B, healthcare, services) AND a workflow marketplace contributing to expansion.
rubrics.sg_localisation_depth: 0 — US/EU-first, no SG content. 1 — Generic 'Singapore' page, no native integration. 2 — One SG mechanic native (e.g. Xero SG OR PayNow OR GST). 3 — Two SG mechanics native. 4 — Three to four SG mechanics native (Xero SG, PayNow, GST, IRAS or MOM/CPF). 5 — All five (Xero SG, PayNow, GST, IRAS, MOM/CPF) native + InvoiceNow Peppol-ready + PDPA-by-default + Model AI GF 2.0 alignment.
rubrics.deployment_speed: 0 — Quarter-long (12+ weeks) implementation. 1 — 6–8 weeks. 2 — 3–5 weeks. 3 — 1–2 weeks. 4 — Live in 48–72 hours with founder/CSM hand-holding. 5 — Same-day live demo + 48 hour production go-live + 2 hour onboarding video for the SME team.
rubrics.founder_led_delivery: 0 — Junior-only delivery / chatbot-only support. 1 — Senior on pitch, junior on delivery (Big-4 SI pattern). 2 — Mixed; senior available but not default. 3 — Senior-led pitch + dedicated CSM but no founder access. 4 — Founder-led setup for every customer + named CSM through the engagement. 5 — Founder-led setup + named Elitez Group senior partner relationship + quarterly executive review.
rubrics.whatsapp_native_workflows: 0 — No WhatsApp integration. 1 — WhatsApp via 3rd-party glue (Twilio / Wati). 2 — WhatsApp Business API native in 1 workflow. 3 — Native in 2–3 workflows. 4 — Native in 4–5 workflows + opinionated WhatsApp UX patterns shipped. 5 — Native in 5+ workflows + WhatsApp templating + Meta-approved message-template management in-product.
rubrics.regional_rollout_reach: 0 — SG only. 1 — SG + 1 manual workaround country. 2 — SG + MY native. 3 — SG + MY + ID with country-specific tax templates. 4 — Five+ SEA countries with native multi-jurisdiction tax / employment engine. 5 — Global (Deel/Zapier-class) with 100+ countries native.

segments.name: HR-heavy SG SME (50–150 staff)
segments.descriptor: F&B groups, services, recruitment, retail with HR exec at burnout. JR+ Archetype A target.

segments.name: Growth-stage SG SME (80–250)
segments.descriptor: Post-Series-A, retention crisis, prepping for B/exit. JR+ Archetype B target.

segments.name: Mid-market SME (150–500)
segments.descriptor: Gen-2 family / pre-IPO / pre-PE-liquidity transformation budget. JR+ Archetype C target.

segments.name: Ops-heavy SME (logistics, security, construction)
segments.descriptor: 20–200 staff, paper-first ops, high admin time on dispatch + scheduling + docs.

segments.name: Professional-services firm (legal / accounting / consulting)
segments.descriptor: Document-heavy SG firm, 30–150 staff, partner-led; AI for drafting / extraction / review.

segments.name: Micro SG SME / sole prop (1–10 staff)
segments.descriptor: Founders + 1–3 employees, self-serve buyer, sub-S$500/mo budget. PSG-track only.

competitors.name: NCS
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: NCS holds JR+ track via parent Singtel; price floor S$50k+ project shuts out sub-150-staff founder-CEO budgets entirely.

competitors.name: Kthology
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Kthology JR+-eligible boutique; founder-led delivery good but no pre-built workflow library — every engagement is bespoke build at S$8–15k entry.

competitors.name: Accenture SG
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Accenture JR+-aware but enterprise (1,000+ headcount) ICP; SME at 50–150 staff is structurally off-spec, no SME pricing track.

competitors.name: JustLogin
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: JustLogin PSG-listed for HR + payroll; but no pre-built AI workflows beyond chatbot — buyer still hires a separate consultant for the redesign.

competitors.name: OTG Lab
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: OTG Lab PSG-listed in chatbot category; doesn't ship multi-workflow back-office bundle the HR-heavy SME actually needs.

competitors.name: Kthology
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Kthology not PSG-listed; engagements priced quote-on-call without grant fluency, lands above S$15k effective ceiling.

competitors.name: OTG Lab
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: OTG Lab ships pre-built chatbot scenarios but only 1 product line; no HR / finance / ops workflow library to attach into.

competitors.name: JustLogin
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: JustLogin's PSG bundle includes leave + payroll workflows but they're configuration-heavy SaaS, not agentic AI workflows.

competitors.name: Zapier
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Zapier's 7,000+ app catalog is not a pre-built workflow — it's a build-your-own kit; SG SMEs without an ops person stall after 2 Zaps.

competitors.name: Make
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Make's visual canvas same shape as Zapier — DIY-first; SG SME founder-CEO buyer cannot self-build production workflows.

competitors.name: n8n
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: n8n requires a developer to self-host; SG SME founder-CEO at 50–150 staff has no developer — workflow library is irrelevant.

competitors.name: Kthology
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Kthology SG-local boutique builds bespoke; no pre-built workflow library, every customer is a custom build engagement.

competitors.name: JustLogin
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: JustLogin SG-built, IRAS / MOM / CPF / PayNow native — 30+ years of SG payroll context; gold standard for SG localisation in HR space.

competitors.name: NCS
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: NCS as Singtel-owned SG SI has deepest GovTech + IRAS/IM8 cybersecurity baseline; price point keeps it out of SME tier though.

competitors.name: Kthology
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Kthology SG boutique with founder-led local context; 4/5 because compliance work is project-by-project, not productised.

competitors.name: OTG Lab
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: OTG Lab IMDA PSG-pre-approved with PDPA / IM8 baseline; but compliance is chatbot-scoped, not multi-workflow.

attack_plans.icp: Founder-CEO of a 50–150 staff SG SME (F&B groups, services, recruitment, retail, security) with a single overworked HR exec, an admin replacement looming, MOM COMPASS frustration, and zero awareness that a JR+ engagement could be 70% subsidised. Often SBF / SCCCI member firms 10–25 years old.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~3,500 SG SMEs sit in the 50–150 staff × workforce-pain-vertical band where JR+ funding is plausible (DOS SME Profile 2024 + WSG eligibility filters). At ~25% Y3 cumulative penetration × S$30k gross Archetype A engagement (~S$9k nett post-grant) = ~875 firms × S$30k = S$26.25M cumulative gross GMV; conservatively rounded to S$27M.
attack_plans.why_gap: Zero competing AI-workflow vendor in the SG SME tier holds WSG JR+ pre-approval. Big-4 / SI (NCS, Accenture) are too expensive and enterprise-shaped; SG boutiques (Kthology) are JR+-eligible but bespoke-build with no pre-built workflow library; chatbot PSG vendors (OTG Lab, JustLogin) don't reach back-office HR redesign. The combination of JR+ pre-approval + 9 pre-built workflows + Elitez Group senior partner backing has no direct match.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Elitez Group's WSG JR+ pre-approved consultant LOA (valid through 17 Mar 2027) gives XinceAI a 12–18 month structural moat. The 9 pre-built workflows let the founder-CEO see a JRET-measurable role redesign in 3 months at S$9–15k nett — the only quote in the market that lands inside the SG SME 'approve without board escalation' band AND ships production tech AND produces JRET evidence.

gtm.pricing: S$30,000 list / ~S$9,000 nett after JR+ 70% subsidy. Anchored against 'less than 3 months of one HR exec hire.'
gtm.channel: Elitez Group warm intros (350-staff client + partner network) + SBF / SCCCI / SHRI BD events + LinkedIn outbound to SG SME founder-CEOs in target verticals.
gtm.pitch: 'Redesign one HR role to JRET-measurable uplift in 3 months, ship 3 production workflows, and the SG government pays 70% of the bill.'
gtm.content: Case-study one-pager per vertical (F&B groups, security services, retail, recruitment) showing JRET pre/post deltas. Free 30-min Diagnostic with the role-cost displacement worksheet. JR+ stacker-math one-pager showing the post-grant nett price.

attack_plans.niche_name: Pre-built workflow library for SG SMEs
attack_plans.icp: SG SME founder-CEO or COO of a 20–200 staff firm who has tried Zapier / Make / n8n / ChatGPT individually and stalled — typical evidence is a half-built Zap, a paused Make scenario, or a ChatGPT subscription nobody uses. Wants a self-serve PSG entry product at S$300–500/mo with no DIY-build ceiling.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~6,500 SG SMEs in the 20–200 headcount × workflow-pain band have at least one digital touchpoint and budget for a S$3.6–6k/yr SaaS workflow product (Glints SEA SMB Tech Spend Survey 2025 median S$5,800/yr). At ~10% Y3 cumulative penetration × S$6k average ARPU = ~650 firms × S$6k = S$3.9M annual recurring; multiplied by 10× book-of-business-LTV multiplier for total addressable lifetime opportunity = ~S$39M.
attack_plans.why_gap: Zapier / Make / n8n are DIY-first by architecture; pre-built libraries are an after-thought. SG-local boutiques (Kthology) build bespoke. WhatsApp BSPs (Respond.io, Wati) ship no HR / finance / ops back-office library. Big-4 / SI build custom platforms, never pre-built. No vendor in the SG market currently ships 9 pre-built agentic workflows priced for self-serve SG SME purchase.
attack_plans.why_we_win: 9 production workflows shipped (HR contracts, onboarding, resume builder, timesheet OCR, WhatsApp scheduler, doc validation, daily SOA checker, receipt claims, commission verification) with SG-native compliance built in. Founder-led 30-min setup means SG SMEs without an ops engineer never stall. PSG-listing pursuit Q3 2026 closes the only gap (effective price after grant) the buyer cares about.

gtm.pricing: S$300–500/mo entry · S$3.6–6k/yr · ~S$1.8–3k/yr nett after PSG 50% subsidy. No setup fee. Single-workflow buyer can attach more workflows at S$100/mo each.
gtm.channel: Self-serve product-led signup via xinceai.sg/free-diagnostic + Elitez Group inbound + SCCCI / SBF events + SG founder podcast tour + LinkedIn organic.
gtm.pitch: '9 ready-to-deploy workflows that speak Xero SG, PayNow, IRAS, MOM and CPF natively. No engineer needed. Live in 48 hours. PSG-eligible.'
gtm.content: Workflow-of-the-week landing pages, each with a 5-minute video demo + 2-minute setup + a free trial. Comparison page vs. Zapier / Make / n8n leading with the pre-built library + grant fluency. Founder-led monthly webinar walking through one workflow per session.

attack_plans.icp: Gen-2 successor (age 28–45) actively running a 80–300 staff SG family-owned SME (manufacturing, F&B groups, healthcare clinic chains, logistics, security services). Patriarch still chairs but the successor controls 2026 tech budget. JRET / HCDT discussion has happened but the firm has no in-house grant lead; family wants 'one trusted partner.'
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: ~640 SG family-owned SMEs in the 80–300 staff band with an active Gen-2 successor and a transformation-ready balance sheet (Stephen Riady Centre / NUS Family Firm Survey 2024 cross-tab + DOS SME Profile 2024 family-firm classification). At ~25% Y3 cumulative penetration × S$120k gross Archetype C engagement = ~160 firms × S$120k = S$19.2M cumulative gross GMV; rounded to S$21M including modest Archetype B upsell mix.
attack_plans.why_gap: Big-4 (KPMG, PwC, Deloitte, EY) and SI (NCS, Accenture) all chase enterprise (1,000+ headcount). SG family-firm advisory at the 80–300-staff band is fragmented across boutiques (Kthology, Mazars Family Office, BoardRoom Succession) — none of which combine workforce-redesign tech, JR+ funding fluency, and a named senior partner who has shipped multiple Gen-2 engagements. The Gen-2 buyer says 'I want one partner for the next 12 months' and finds no obvious answer.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Elitez Group's 350-staff SG operation, Council Member presence at SBF / SCCCI / SHRI, and JR+ pre-approved consultant status make 'one trusted partner' credible. The full XinceAI workforce tech stack + JRET / HCDT outcome guarantee + Gen-2-friendly modernisation narrative ('show your dad the modern option, here's what your peers are doing') wins where Big-4 over-shoots and boutiques under-serve.

gtm.pricing: S$80,000–150,000 gross / S$24,000–45,000 nett after JR+ + EDG + SFEC stack. 12-month engagement with milestone billing.
gtm.channel: Elitez Group senior-partner relationship-led outreach + SBF / SCCCI Council Member peer introductions + NUS / NTU alumni networks (Gen-2 LinkedIn outreach) + named Big-4 / boutique partner referral motion ('we take the SME bottom-half of your funnel').
gtm.pitch: 'One trusted partner for your 12-month workforce transformation: the workforce strategy, the change management, and the production tech — at one-quarter the cost of Big-4, with the JRET / HCDT proof artefacts your investors will ask for.'
gtm.content: Two-pager 'Gen-2 modernisation playbook' showing 3 anonymised SG case studies with JRET / HCDT before/after deltas. Quarterly Family Business Modernisation roundtable hosted by Elitez Group (small-format, peer-to-peer). Direct LinkedIn outreach + Substack thought-leadership tagged to NUS Family Business Network audience.
